title: no FSM in design, execute "synth" without "-nofsm", some logic is recognized as FSM and the number of register will become more
body:
**Version**
Yosys 0.33

**which OS**
macOS

**Verilog Code**
```
assign in_new = in & (~(in-1));
assign covert = onehot2bin(in_new);
assign out = 1'b1 << out_pre;
always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        out_pre <= 3'b110;
    end
    else if (|in) begin
        out_pre <= covert;
    end
end

function integer onehot2bin(input integer onehot);
    integer i;
    onehot2bin = 0;
    for (i=0; i<7; i=i+1) begin
        if (onehot[i]) onehot2bin = i;
    end
endfunction
```

**Yosys script**
```
read_verilog -sv test.v
hierarchy -top test
synth -top test <-nofsm>
opt -purge
techmap
dfflibmap -liberty mycells.lib
opt -undriven
abc -liberty mycells.lib 
tee -a area.log stat -liberty mycells.lib
```

**area report**
with -nofsm
```
Number of wires:                 87
   Number of wire bits:             99
   Number of public wires:           6
   Number of public wire bits:      18
   Number of memories:               0
   Number of memory bits:            0
   Number of processes:              0
   Number of cells:                 39
     DFF_PN0                         1
     DFF_PN1                         2
     NAND                           12
     NOR                            15
     NOT                             9
   Chip area for module '\test': 195.000000
```
without -nofsm
```
Number of wires:                150
   Number of wire bits:            168
   Number of public wires:           5
   Number of public wire bits:      19
   Number of memories:               0
   Number of memory bits:            0
   Number of processes:              0
   Number of cells:                 70
     DFF_PN0                         6
     DFF_PN1                         3
     NAND                           33
     NOR                            15
     NOT                            13
   Chip area for module '\test': 411.000000
```

**log**
```
3.7.2. Executing FSM_EXTRACT pass (extracting FSM from design).
Extracting FSM `\out_pre' from module `\test'.
  found $adff cell for state register: $procdff$45
  root of input selection tree: $0\out_pre[2:0]
  found reset state: 3'110 (from async reset)
  found ctrl input: $procmux$44_CMP
  found ctrl input: \in_new [6]
  found ctrl input: \in_new [5]
  found ctrl input: \in_new [4]
  found ctrl input: \in_new [3]
  found ctrl input: \in_new [2]
  found ctrl input: \in_new [1]
  found state code: 3'000
  found state code: 3'001
  found state code: 3'010
  found state code: 3'011
  found state code: 3'100
  found state code: 3'101
  ctrl inputs: { $procmux$44_CMP \in_new [6:1] }
  ctrl outputs: $0\out_pre[2:0]
  transition:      3'000 7'0------ ->      3'000 3'000
  transition:      3'000 7'1000000 ->      3'000 3'000
  transition:      3'000 7'1000001 ->      3'001 3'001
...
```

**confusion**
in my design, only 3-bits register. when without "-nofsm", some logic is recognized as FSM and optimize, but the register number becomes 9 and add more combination, the area becomes larger. is this normal?
